pattern: Ten rods, three colors -- bucket each ring by its rod digit into a small set, then count the rods whose set reached all three colors.
hints: The string is a flat sequence of (color, rod-digit) pairs -- read it two characters at a time.
Give every rod (there are only ten, 0-9) its own bucket, and drop each ring's color into its rod's bucket.
A rod qualifies once its bucket holds all three distinct colors, so a set per rod (not a count) is exactly what you need.

edge_inputs.description: one rod gets all three colors, others partial
no rod ever completes all three colors
smallest possible input, a single ring
every ring lands on the same rod, all colors present
same color repeated on a rod, never reaches three distinct colors
two rods each miss exactly one color
all ten rods present but only rod 0 completes the set

stress.description: random colors and rod digits across the max n=100 rings
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = 100
    chars = []
    for _ in range(n):
        chars.append(rng.choice('RGB'))
        chars.append(rng.choice('0123456789'))
    return [''.join(chars)]
